other_observers: None; David Silverman was original observer, and there were no other observers
specific_location: east end of Greenhorn Meadows Park along Greenhorn Creek
description: This bird looked like a Warbling Vireo, except for very yellow underparts. It was about same size as nearby Warbling Vireo, but perhaps a bit smaller.  CROWN & FACE: Crown was grayish. White supercilium was flanked below by dark eyeline extending thru lores. Eye was dark.  BILL & LEGS: Bill was dark, short, thicker than warbler with small hook at tip. Legs were dark.  UPPERPARTS: Brownish olive-green with darker contrasting slaty colored wings. There were no wingbars on this bird.  UNDERPARTS: Practically solid yellow, which is typical of many fall-plumaged birds. It was slightly less yellow on belly, but had a very bright yellow breast.Behaviors: Bird sluggishly foraged in willows and narrow-leaf cottonwoods with other vireos & warblers.Call: None heardPlumage: Fall plumage
